sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a family history of hypertension and states he has experienced secondary hypertension for the past six months. He also reports feeling lightheaded when rising quickly from a seated position. The patient denies chest pain, shortness of breath, or palpitations.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure is 138/82 mmHg. Heart rate is 78 bpm and regular. Neurological exam is unremarkable.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's symptoms and blood pressure readings are consistent with secondary hypertension. A differential diagnosis includes renal artery stenosis and medication side effects.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Order a metabolic panel to evaluate electrolytes and renal function. Consider referral to a nephrology specialist for evaluation of renal artery stenosis. Initiate spironolactone 25mg daily and monitor electrolytes closely. Schedule a follow-up appointment in two weeks to assess response to treatment.
